summary: The severity of obstructive sleep apnea is associated with increased hypoxemia and progression to respiratory failure, which raises the likelihood of requiring advanced airway support including high continuous positive airway pressure. Clinical pathways indicate that worse disease stages correlate with higher needs for ventilatory assistance, supporting this predictive relationship.
